message:
feat(focus): unify deferred focus targeting when host is blurred

When the terminal loses focus (host_focused=false), focus mutations
(focus(), focus_next(), focus_back(), navigate()) previously wrote
directly to self.current, emitting FocusGained events even though the
host wasn't focused. This created inconsistencies where the widget tree
thought it had focus while the terminal did not.

This change introduces a unified active_focus_target() accessor that
returns the current focus ID if the host is focused, or the pending
deferred target if not. All focus mutation paths now:

1. Check active_focus_target() instead of self.current for "where is
   focus now" queries (set_focus_explicit, navigate, focus_next/prev,
   focus_back)
2. Route through set_focus_target() which auto-defers to
   set_pending_focus_target() when host is blurred, storing the target
   in pending_focus_on_host_gain without setting self.current
3. Emit FocusLost immediately when deferring (the old target loses
   focus) but defer FocusGained until apply_host_focus(true)

Also removes the now-redundant focus_target_on_host_gain() method —
deferred_focus_target() now delegates to active_focus_target() which
handles both current and pending states.

Adds tests:
- focus_while_host_blurred_updates_deferred_target_without_restoring_current
- focus_next_while_host_blurred_advances_deferred_target
- focus_call_while_host_blurred_defers_until_focus_gain (modal integration)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
